description_long: This object is a vintage film projector, characterized by its dual large, circular film reels mounted on top, which are black and metallic in appearance. The main body is a robust, boxy structure made of metal, featuring various knobs, dials, and a prominent hand crank on one side. The front section houses multiple cylindrical lenses and viewports, with a metallic and glass construction, indicating a multi-lens turret system for different focal lengths. The side view reveals a complex arrangement of mechanical components, including a winding handle and several protruding elements for manual operation. The rear of the projector displays a flat panel with a viewing window and additional mechanical details, while the overall finish is a mix of matte and slightly glossy metallic surfaces. The object exudes an industrial, utilitarian aesthetic typical of mid-20th-century professional film equipment.
description: A vintage dual-reel film projector with a metal body, multiple lenses, and a hand crank, featuring a robust and industrial design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual film reels, lens turret, and hand crank prominently.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical lenses, winding handle, and mechanical components.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat panel, viewing window, and the backs of the film reels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the hand crank and film reel mechanisms visible.
attribute_lens_type: interchangeable; multiple cylindrical lenses with adjustment rings visible
attribute_control_panel_design: A set of mechanical dials and knobs located on the front and side, with no digital interface or touch controls; labeling is minimal or absent.
